g.text: HTTP/2 Multiplexing -- one TCP, many concurrent streams
Same 5 calls. HTTP/1.1 pays 5 TLS handshakes serialized; HTTP/2 pays 1, then runs 5 streams in parallel.
Each call: open TCP, TLS handshake, request, response, close. Repeat.
5 TLS handshakes (= 15 RTTs) before the last call returns
Single TLS handshake. 5 streams share the connection, each independently long-lived.
<-- connection stays open the entire time -->
1 TLS handshake (= 3 RTTs) -- regardless of how many calls follow
